help: Carbon intensities of grid electricity and gasoline. These are true uncertainty priors bounded by evidence: the range reflects real measurement disagreement and methodological choice (operational-only vs life-cycle-inclusive for low-carbon generation; NGCC vs coal for fossil). HIGH is rejected for every L1 emission factor because widening beyond the evidence-anchored range is not defensible.

parameters.label: CO2 intensity of low-carbon generation
parameters.physical_meaning: Lifecycle or operational CO2 per kWh of low-carbon electricity. Low-carbon technologies cluster in the tens of gCO2e/kWh: wind ~10-20, solar ~40-50, hydro ~20-60, nuclear ~10-20. The triangular prior spans operational-only (~0.02 kg/kWh) to life-cycle-inclusive (~0.08 kg/kWh).
parameters.citation: NREL, Life Cycle Greenhouse Gas Emissions from Electricity Generation: Update (2021), NREL/FS-6A50-80580. See also UNECE LCA of Electricity Generation Technologies (2022).
parameters.why_default_free: Operational-only (~0.02-0.03) vs life-cycle-inclusive (~0.06-0.08) is a real methodological choice the reader should see. LOW trims the life-cycle tail; MEDIUM retains it for paper reproduction.
parameters.why_high_rejected: Widening beyond life-cycle-inclusive is not supported by NREL or UNECE evidence.
parameters.design_note: A region-specific state-weighted mixture prior would be more accurate but requires solving the clean-gen-mix at each time step. Current triangular is a reasonable aggregate simplification anchored by NREL lifecycle ranges.

parameters.label: CO2 intensity of fossil generation
parameters.physical_meaning: CO2 per kWh of fossil-fuel-fired electricity. Natural gas combined-cycle (NGCC) emits ~0.35-0.45 kg/kWh; coal plants emit ~0.9-1.2 kg/kWh; oil ~0.6-0.8 kg/kWh. The prior is region-specific: California's fossil fleet is gas-dominated (narrow, lower); Ohio's includes a substantial coal share (broader, heavier upper tail).
parameters.citation: NREL, Life Cycle Greenhouse Gas Emissions from Electricity Generation: Update (2021). EIA state electricity profiles for regional fuel mix.
parameters.why_default_free: Real technology range. California: narrow gas-dominated fossil prior (0.38-0.55 low). Ohio: gas+coal generation-weighted prior recalibrated in v5.1.7 to mode 0.54 = 0.43*0.50 (gas) + 0.34*0.95 (coal) per EIA 2024 Ohio mix; previous 0.62 mode reflected an unstated marginal-emission-factor assumption. Low bound 0.40 = peak gas dispatch; high bound 0.75 = peak coal dispatch.
parameters.why_high_rejected: No fossil technology lies outside the NGCC-coal span.

parameters.label: CO2-equivalent intensity for gasoline
parameters.physical_meaning: kg CO2 per kWh-equivalent of gasoline combustion. Derived from EPA: 8.887 kg CO2 per gallon of gasoline (EPA Emission Factors for Greenhouse Gas Inventories, 2024) and EIA: ~33.7 kWh (lower heating value) per gallon. With an onboard ICE+alternator conversion efficiency of ~15%, 1 kWh of usable vehicle electricity requires ~0.714 L of gasoline, producing ~1.65 kg CO2.
parameters.citation: EPA, Emission Factors for Greenhouse Gas Inventories (2024), Table 2. EIA, Energy Units and Calculators. See also: ICE thermal efficiency ~30% (Springer, doi:10.1007/978-981-16-1582-5_6); alternator efficiency typically <50% (MDPI Machines 2022, doi:10.3390/machines10060478).
parameters.why_default_free: Tight physical range derived from EPA CO2/gallon and EIA energy content; LOW captures well-to-wheel vs tank-to-wheel convention difference.
parameters.why_medium_rejected: Previous MEDIUM (1.45-1.85) extended beyond the EPA+EIA-derived range without evidence.
